statement.markdown: The n-queens puzzle is the problem of placing `n` queens on an `n x n` chessboard such that no two queens attack each other. Given the value `n,` return the total number of distinct solutions.
statement.inputFormat: Input is a positive integer `n` representing the size of the chessboard.
statement.outputFormat: The function should return an integer representing the total number of distinct solutions.
statement.notes: A valid placement of queens is defined as any arrangement of queens on the board such that no two queens attack each other.

examples.explanation: Explanation:  The output is the total number of distinct solutions for placing 4 queens on a 4x4 chessboard.

hints.text: Check if a given queen is under attack from any other queen

editorial.title: N-Queens Backtracking Problem Editorial
editorial.intuition: The problem can be solved by using backtracking, where we try to place each queen on the board and remove it when a conflict is detected.
editorial.bruteForce: A brute force approach would involve trying all possible placements of queens on the board and checking if they are valid.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution involves using backtracking to try all possible placements of queens on the board and removing it when a conflict is detected.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The correctness of the solution can be proven by showing that all possible valid placements of queens on the board are covered by the backtracking approach.
editorial.dryRun: Dry run of the optimal solution
